page: Amazon Web Services m6a.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores with hyper-threading operating at 3.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6a.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving a top-tier rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU encryption tests. General multi-core processing, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing all register in the top 25% tier. However, single-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth fall into the average category, indicating that workloads relying heavily on single-threaded execution or massive sequential memory transfers may experience moderate performance relative to specialized hardware.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m6a.24xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise-scale general-purpose workloads. Its high core count and strong multi-core performance make it highly effective for database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing and text generation with smaller models. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs positions this instance as a cost-effective choice for compute-heavy and memory-balanced applications that do not require local NVMe speeds or hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core and a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier multi-core CPU capabilities and encryption speeds, alongside strong database operations and software compilation performance. Conversely, single-core execution and large-block memory bandwidth remain average. This resource profile makes the m6a.24xlarge a highly capable and cost-efficient option for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database operations, and general-purpose workloads requiring substantial parallel processing capacity without the need for local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance delivering high-density multi-core AMD EPYC performance and substantial DDR4 memory for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
Equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Delivers 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Achieves top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption benchmark performance
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded workloads
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, optimizing cost for network-reliant tasks
